skill.name: google-official-seo-guide
skill.description: Use official Google Search documentation to answer SEO, crawling, indexing, Search Console, structured data, and rich result questions.
skill.summary: Apply official Google Search guidance to technical SEO planning, audits, and implementation decisions.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown documentation snippets and official Google example URLs, not executable skill code. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, hidden downloader, or malicious command runner was found. Residual risk is low because some examples mention local tunnels, HTTP tools, fetch, and innerHTML, so users should treat generated implementation advice as guidance to review.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs, example IP addresses, fetch calls, FTP references, and ngrok tunnel text are copied documentation examples for SEO testing and Google Search behavior. They do not run automatically and do not transmit user data from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown documentation or fenced examples. I found no code path that makes outbound requests during skill activation.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Findings Are Fenced Markdown Samples
low_findings.description: The shell and command patterns are static examples such as a local Python HTTP server, ngrok, DNS lookup commands, and structured data snippets. They are not invoked by a script or automation in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear inside Markdown code fences and explanatory documentation. No executable file, hook, installer, or runtime wrapper was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Hits Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are caused by SEO terms, metadata examples, verification tokens, URLs, and network troubleshooting documentation. They do not implement cryptography, scanning, or system discovery logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged lines are natural-language guidance or HTML examples. There is no hashing API, crypto library call, recon loop, or data collection routine.

low_findings.title: JavaScript Examples Require Implementation Review
low_findings.description: Some reference examples include fetch and innerHTML patterns that could be unsafe if copied into production without validation. In this skill they are inert documentation, but users should review generated implementation code before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are real JavaScript examples, but they are not executed by the skill. The residual risk is limited to unsafe reuse by a user or model response.

content.user_title: Improve SEO with Google Search Guidance
content.value_statement: Technical SEO decisions can be hard to align with Google Search guidance. This skill uses official Google documentation to support audits, implementation plans, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Explains Google Search crawling, indexing, and serving concepts from reference material.
Guides structured data decisions for VideoObject, BroadcastEvent, Clip, breadcrumbs, and other rich results.
Helps troubleshoot robots.txt, noindex, canonical URLs, redirects, sitemaps, and crawl access issues.
Summarizes Search Console report usage for developers, SEO specialists, and marketers.
Reviews content, link, mobile-first, image, video, and spam policy guidance from included references.
Provides practical checklists for site migrations, international SEO, JavaScript SEO, and search appearance improvements.
limitations: It does not connect to Google Search Console or fetch live site data by itself.
It cannot guarantee rankings, indexing, rich result eligibility, or traffic improvements.
The bundled documentation may become outdated when Google changes its guidance.
Implementation advice still needs review against the target site, CMS, and legal requirements.

use_cases.title: Audit a Website Launch
use_cases.description: Check crawlability, canonical tags, redirects, sitemaps, structured data, mobile readiness, and indexing controls before launch.

use_cases.title: Plan Search Appearance Improvements
use_cases.description: Identify practical changes for title links, snippets, breadcrumbs, images, videos, rich results, and site names.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Traffic or Indexing Issues
use_cases.description: Use Google documentation to interpret common Search Console issues and prepare questions for engineering teams.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an SEO Concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how Google Search crawling, indexing, and serving work. Use simple terms and include actions I can take on a small website.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Page for SEO Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page plan for Google SEO basics: [paste page type, title, meta description, headings, links, and media]. List issues and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Structured Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose structured data for this content: [describe content]. Include required fields, validation steps, and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Technical Search Problems
prompt_templates.prompt: I have this Search Console issue: [paste issue]. Build a debugging plan using crawling, indexing, canonical, robots, redirect, and sitemap checks.

output_examples.input: A product page is indexed, but the title shown in Google is different from the page title.
output_examples.output: The skill would explain title link generation factors, then suggest checking the visible page heading, title element, internal anchor text, duplicate titles, and snippet quality.

output_examples.input: A video page needs better visibility in Google Search.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline video indexing requirements, stable video and thumbnail URLs, a dedicated watch page, structured data options, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A site migration caused fewer indexed pages.
output_examples.output: The skill would propose checks for redirects, canonical signals, robots rules, sitemaps, Search Console reports, and crawl access changes.

best_practices: Start with the relevant reference section, then ask for a checklist tied to your page type or issue.
Provide concrete details such as URLs, templates, CMS limits, Search Console messages, and launch timelines.
Validate structured data, robots rules, redirects, and indexing changes with official tools before release.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to promise rankings, traffic increases, or guaranteed rich results.
Do not copy example code into production without security, accessibility, and platform review.
Do not treat robots.txt as a complete way to remove private or sensitive content from Search.

faq.question: Does this skill use official Google documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The included references are organized from Google Search documentation and cover fundamentals, crawling, indexing, appearance, and policies.

faq.question: Can it access my Search Console account?
faq.answer: No. It can explain reports and workflows, but it does not authenticate to Google or retrieve account data.

faq.question: Can it guarantee that Google will index my page?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend compliant practices, but Google controls crawling, indexing, serving, and rich result display.

faq.question: Can it help with JavaScript SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance on rendering, crawlable links, HTTP status handling, canonical tags, and lazy-loaded content.

faq.question: Is it useful for structured data?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers schema.org guidance, video structured data, breadcrumbs, rich result eligibility, and validation practices.

faq.question: How should teams use the output?
faq.answer: Use it as an implementation guide and audit checklist. Confirm final changes with official testing tools and site-specific review.
